Kedrovsky of Kherson reports that the phrase "Glory to Ukraine!" echoes in the hall, then naturally transitions into singing "Ukraine has not yet died!" | The youth group of Ukrainians in front of Mykhailo Hrushevsky's house shouted, "Glory to Ukraine!" the day before the congress. | Hrushevsky responded from the porch, stating: "Long live democratic Russia." | Thousands of chests loudly adopted the phrase "Glory to Ukraine," and the sound resonated powerfully in the air for a long time. | Those who consider themselves conscious Ukrainian citizens and wish to stand under their native yellow-and-blue flag are asked to raise their hands; almost all attendees raised their hands. | Future Prime Minister of the Ukrainian People's Republic, Boris Martos, recalled how the local community in Poltava celebrated this universal document. | A multi-person procession, led by clergy and carrying Ukrainian flags, proceeded to the archiepiscopal house where a wooden church, transported from Zaporizhzhia, stood in the courtyard. | The Deputy Chairman of the Ukrainian General Military Committee recalled meeting with Ukrainian guards protecting the "Winter Palace," stating, "We greeted him (the guard, Yu.Yu.) and heard the response to all 'for the guard's sake': 'Glory to Ukraine!'" | During one and a half to two hours, strong, slender formations marched ceremonially in front of the Center under national flags. | Standing up, the attendees began carrying Petliura, Hrushevsky, and Skoropadsky in their arms while shouting "Glory to Ukraine!" | He even went on attacks against Germans with "planted bayonets and a mighty shout – 'Glory to Ukraine!'". | Sotnyk Ivan Ostrovershenko of the 7th company, 2nd regiment of the Bohdanivsky Regiment, later recalled how a general arrived at the regiment and praised the exemplary discipline, but reacted painfully to the language used within the regiment. | Ah, so this is what you all are thinking about Ukraine; what does that mean? "Glory to the Cossacks!". | After consulting each other, the attendees decided to greet the Leader according to UVO ideology: when the Colonel entered the hall, all present were to stand at attention facing the Colonel and shout the nationalist greeting, "Glory to the Leader!"
